question_text:
In commit dceec68 on 2026-07-09, you wrote line 38 of gradebook/analytics.py:
38:     k = int(len(ordered) * p / 100)

Consider changing `len(ordered) * p / 100` to `len(ordered) * p // 100`.

After changing `len(ordered) * p / 100` to `len(ordered) * p // 100`, what percentile results break when that calculation has a fractional value, and why does applying `int()` afterward no longer preserve the original index?
